policy.code_copying: No third-party source files were copied into this repository. The inventory records product and architecture patterns only.
policy.license_handling: MIT and Apache-2.0 projects may inform implementation patterns. Projects without a license or with restrictive terms are used only for behavioral analysis and source-location citation.
policy.mvp_boundary: Borrowed ideas must support the sensor NPN/PNP wiring, PLC input troubleshooting, personal graph, learning path, quiz, and feedback loop.

scanned_projects.name: Asset Intelligence Graph-RAG
scanned_projects.primary_value: Manufacturing-oriented graph RAG, structured entity ingestion, relationship scoring, and graph-aware synthesis.

implemented_features.name: Structured answer with evidence, reasoning, knowledge refs, and follow-up prompts
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The MVP returns deterministic troubleshooting guidance first, then exposes evidence_used, reasoning_steps, knowledge_refs, ability_hits, next_questions, and suggested tools.
verification: Manual browser smoke test shows chat cards and tool suggestions

implemented_features.name: Half-screen explanation for ability, knowledge, task, question, and message
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: A lightweight drawer explains the selected object without leaving the chat or full-screen tool workspace. Explanations record learning events but never override deterministic scores.

implemented_features.name: Rules-first hybrid retrieval over local knowledge and ability links
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The MVP avoids Milvus, Neo4j, MySQL, and BM25 dependencies. It uses cached JSON, keyword matching, ability weighting, pattern signals, and source-preserving results.
verification: JSON parse check covers knowledge and ability files

implemented_features.name: Session-level learner context pack injected into chat and plan generation
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The MVP keeps the profile local to a session and summarizes weak abilities, recent events, confidence, and next best action for prompt injection.

implemented_features.name: Student learning dashboard that turns personal graph evidence into immediate next actions
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The dashboard avoids a new database or model. It summarizes the session personal graph into readiness, risks, focus abilities, today's actions, tool suggestions, and recent evidence.
verification: tests/api_smoke.test.py checks /api/student/dashboard before and after chat events

implemented_features.name: Personalized learning path with stages, today sheet, seven-day plan, resources, and checkpoints
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The plan is generated from the personal graph, job graph, local resources, and training tasks. Video URLs are read only from resources.json; missing resources are reported honestly.
verification: JSON parse check covers resources and tasks

implemented_features.name: Evidence-driven personal ability graph with status, score, confidence, and update reasons
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The graph is recalculated from auditable JSON events and deterministic quiz scoring. Safety abilities keep caution prompts and are not treated as permanently complete.

implemented_features.name: Scenario-based troubleshooting practice that updates the personal graph
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The MVP uses fixed industrial scenarios and deterministic expected evidence checks instead of open-ended roleplay scoring.
verification: tests/api_smoke.test.py checks scenario start and step

implemented_features.name: Job ability graph proposal and confirmation workflow
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The MVP does not crawl or auto-write the formal job graph. It generates proposals from imported materials and requires teacher confirmation before snapshot updates.
verification: tests/api_smoke.test.py checks job graph proposal and confirm endpoints
JSON parse check covers graph proposal data

implemented_features.name: Multi-agent role separation implemented as lightweight services
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The MVP avoids an expensive multi-agent runtime. Each role is a small deterministic service with shared local JSON and a single HTTP API.

implemented_features.name: Full-screen graph workbench with job, personal, and current-problem graph views
source_refs: Asset Intelligence Graph-RAG/frontend/src/App.tsx:377 Compatibility among retrieved parts
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The first version keeps visualization dependency-free: SVG cards, node evidence detail, Mermaid text, and workspace tabs instead of a heavy graph rendering stack.
verification: Mermaid samples use flowchart TD/LR syntax

implemented_features.name: Compliant job intelligence updater that generates teacher-reviewable job graph proposals
source_refs: CrawlerTutorial/docs/爬虫进价/01_工程化爬虫开发规范.md:config, logging, retry, data cleaning guidance
implemented_features.mvp_adaptation: The MVP does not implement social platform crawling, login, cookies, proxies, CAPTCHA handling, anti-detection, or large-scale crawling. It reads explicitly approved local/public sources, extracts deterministic ability evidence, and writes pending proposals for teacher confirmation.

deferred_features.reason: Useful but beyond the student-side MVP. Keep as a later import workflow after the manual knowledge source map is stable.
The current corpus is small. Local JSON and cached retrieval are faster to demo and easier to review.
The MVP explicitly avoids accounts and databases. Personal graphs stay session-level.
DKT/SAKT need real longitudinal data. Deterministic mastery scoring is more auditable for the competition MVP.
Adds latency and unpredictability. The MVP uses modular services and optional LLM explanations instead.
